title: Workers at a Wells Fargo branch just became the first at a major U.S. bank to unionize

title: Wells Fargo employees vote in favor of unionization
text: Dec 20 (Reuters) - Wells Fargo (WFC.N) employees at its Albuquerque, New Mexico branch voted to join a union on Wednesday, opening up a new frontier in the labor campaign against corporate America.

title: Wells Fargo Workers in Albuquerque Vote to Form Union, a First For a Megabank

title: GoodLeap Hires John Shrewsberry as Chief Financial Officer
text: Shrewsberry brings 22 years of experience helping lead Wells Fargo through decades of growth SAN FRANCISCO , Dec. 18, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- GoodLeap, LLC, America's leading digital marketplace for sustainable solutions, today announced it has hired John Shrewsberry as Chief Financial Officer. In his capacity as CFO, Shrewsberry will oversee Financial Planning, Accounting, Capital Markets, Investor Relations, Human Resources, Legal, and Compliance.

title: UnidosUS and Wells Fargo Launch Awareness Program to Reach Unbanked Latinos
text: WASHINGTON , Dec. 14, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- UnidosUS—the nation's largest Latino civil rights and advocacy organization and Wells Fargo are launching a program to promote Bank On nationally certified accounts, transaction accounts that offer low or no fees, no overdraft charges, and other accessible features. The nonprofit Cities for Financial Empowerment Fund (CFE Fund), who leads the national Bank On movement, also will partner as a program advisor and implementing partner.

title: Goldman hires Wells Fargo executive as head of transactional banking

title: Wells Fargo flagged by consumer protection agency for inequitable use of price discrimination
text: Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE:WFC) found itself in hot water with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau last year during an investigation into potentially improper use of loan discounts, according to a report by CNBC.  The probe concerned a practice called price discrimination, which bankers use to offer discounts on mortgages to get deals done.

title: Wells Fargo Names Darlene Goins Head of Philanthropy and Community Impact, President of Wells Fargo Foundation
text: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- #cdfis--Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE:WFC) today announced that Darlene Goins is named head of Philanthropy and Community Impact and president of the Wells Fargo Foundation. She will be responsible for the company's philanthropic strategy in housing access and affordability, financial health, small business growth and sustainability, reporting to vice chairman of Public Affairs Bill Daley. Previously, Goins served as head of Diverse Customer Segments within Wells Fargo.

title: Wells Fargo Layoffs 2024: What to Know About the Latest WFC Job Cuts

title: Wells Fargo CEO expects severance expenses to exceed $750 million
text: Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf told investors on Tuesday he expects to book higher-than-anticipated severance expenses between $750 million to a little less than $1 billion in the fourth quarter.

title: Wells Fargo to book up to nearly $1 billion in Q4 severance costs
text: Wells Fargo & Co.'s WFC, -1.07% stock was down by 0.4% on Tuesday after the bank said it plans to book $750 million to nearly $1 billion in fourth-quarter severance costs. Chief Executive Charlie Scharf said at the Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services conference that the bank has seen turnover come down, which is leading to “more severance than we otherwise would have anticipated.

title: U.S. bank CEOs expected to protest regulation push before Congress

title: Ex-Wells Fargo CEO sues bank for $34 million in withheld pay, stock
text: Former Wells Fargo & Co CEO Tim Sloan filed a lawsuit on Friday accusing the bank of failing to pay him more than $34 million after he resigned in 2019 amid a wide-ranging sales practices scandal.

title: Former CEO Tim Sloan Sues Wells Fargo for $34 Million
text: Former Wells Fargo CEO Tim Sloan has filed a lawsuit against the company, seeking over $34 million in unpaid compensation.  Sloan alleged that Wells Fargo unlawfully withheld his deferred pay and bonus after he stepped down as CEO in 2019, Bloomberg reported Friday (Dec. 1).

title: Wells Fargo (WFC) Aided by Cost Control, Hurt by Lower Revenues
text: Wells Fargo's (WFC) cost-saving measures, like branch closures and headcount reduction, will help offset falling revenue trends due to volatile fee income and NII decline.
